summary: This guidance explains the common documents a W2 borrower is typically asked to provide and what to expect after documents are submitted. It focuses on education and operational next steps, and escalates any rate or qualification questions to a licensed Loan Officer.

key_steps.title: Gather standard identity documents
key_steps.instruction: Prepare a government-issued photo ID and confirm your legal name matches your documents.
key_steps.notes: Do not submit sensitive identifiers (e.g., SSN) through unapproved channels.

key_steps.title: Collect income documentation
key_steps.instruction: Locate your most recent pay stubs and W-2s as requested by your Loan Officer or secure portal.

key_steps.title: Provide asset documentation if requested
key_steps.instruction: If you plan to use funds for closing or down payment, gather recent bank statements for the accounts you’ll use.

key_steps.title: Submit documents through the approved channel
key_steps.instruction: Upload documents through the lender’s secure portal or method provided by your Loan Officer. Avoid email or text unless explicitly instructed.
key_steps.notes: Submitting through the correct channel reduces delays and re-requests.

key_steps.title: Confirm receipt and next steps
key_steps.instruction: After submission, confirm the documents were received and ask what the next milestone is (e.g., initial review, appraisal scheduling).

faq_items.question: Why do I need to provide pay stubs and W-2s?
faq_items.answer: These documents help verify employment and income consistency. The exact documents requested can vary by lender process and your situation, and your Loan Officer will confirm what applies to you.

faq_items.question: Can I email my documents?
faq_items.answer: It’s usually best to use the secure portal or method your Loan Officer provides. If you’re unsure which method to use, ask your Loan Officer for the preferred secure option.

faq_items.question: How long does the document review take?
faq_items.answer: Timelines vary based on volume and completeness. A common way to avoid delays is submitting clear, complete documents and responding quickly if additional items are requested.

escalation_triggers.trigger_text: What rate can I get? / What APR will I qualify for?
Do I qualify? / Will I be approved?
My income is $X and my credit score is Y...
